scenario_results.reason: Returned an in-scope capital-markets analytics package with explicit metric logic, benchmark context, source hierarchy, caveats, and thresholds tied to named records.
Tracking-error ambiguity was preserved as a metric-definition dispute with named alternative methods instead of a false single number.
Stale benchmark constituents, missing prices, and unresolved corporate actions triggered blocked-on-prerequisites handling instead of implied publishability.
OMS, IBOR, and fund-accounting disagreement was preserved as a reconciliation conflict with source lineage rather than collapsed into one answer.
Unsupported causal language was refused and downgraded to descriptive analysis boundaries.
Capital-markets terms such as composite return, excess return, spread duration, DV01, slippage, and liquidity buckets were normalized into artifact-aware metric definitions.
Portfolio rebalance and execution-tactic requests were refused and routed to investment or execution owners.
Disclosure-support work stopped when extracted-performance and benchmark caveats were missing, with escalation to the proper approval chain.
TRACE timing and OMS timestamp disagreement was preserved as a fixed-income execution-quality conflict instead of a definitive score.
Tenant-specific benchmark adaptation was recorded with the preserved public baseline and explicit comparability caveats.
Missing benchmark owner, valuation hierarchy, currency policy, share-class rules, and audience context returned blocked-on-prerequisites status with required retrieval hooks.
Requests for NAV-supporting signoff and publication approval were refused and routed to valuation, compliance, or client-reporting approvers.

notes: Scenario coverage validates metric-definition control, report assembly, unsupported-claim refusal, disclosure-risk handling, cross-system conflict handling, and adjacent-lane routing behavior.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities plus analytics-reporting-and-disclosure and cross-industry requirements, and every scenario is represented in scenario coverage.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
